name: Lisbon

palette.name: Limestone Cream
palette.rationale: Warm limestone facades and sunlit paved squares anchor central Lisbon's material palette.

palette.name: Azulejo Blue
palette.rationale: Blue azulejo tiles are one of Lisbon's most recognizable repeatable design references.

palette.name: Calçada Light
palette.rationale: Pale calçada paving and sun glare create a useful neutral for plazas and pedestrian surfaces.

palette.name: Tram Yellow
palette.rationale: Historic yellow trams provide a clear transport accent without claiming civic official status.

palette.name: Tile Red
palette.rationale: Red ceramic roofs and warm tile edges add a compact skyline accent.

palette.name: Tagus Blue
palette.rationale: A deeper river blue links the palette to the Tagus and waterfront viewpoints.

palette.name: Miradouro Green
palette.rationale: Gardened overlooks and hilltop planting add a secondary green layer to the stone-and-tile system.

sources.title: Lisboa
sources.note: Used for civic and city background context.

sources.title: Azulejos in Lisbon
sources.note: Used to support interpretation of tile-driven color identity.

notes: Complete seed record; all colors are editorial or source-informed interpretations, with no official city color claim.
editorialSummary: Lisbon's complete starter palette layers limestone, azulejo blue, calçada light, tram yellow, roof tile, Tagus water, and garden green into a designer-readable city system.
